title: Parallax Model Descriptor (m-descriptor)
description: Canonical serialized m-descriptor input to m-metamodel. This revision covers persistence, optional conventional column mappings, pkGeneration, the defining/reverse relationship union, indices, asOfAxes, closed-tree inheritance, and recursively typed value objects with One/Many multiplicity.

entities.description: Two or more entities mapped in one descriptor (so relationships can name sibling entities). Mutually exclusive with the single `entity` form.

neutralType.description: Canonical m-descriptor spelling of an m-core NeutralType variant (m-descriptor "Type spellings"). The decimal spelling carries precision/scale, e.g. decimal(18,2). The `json` type is the embedded-value type a valueObject element maps to; m-dialect maps it to each dialect's structured-document column type.

asOfAxes.description: The supported temporal shapes are one transactionTime axis or validTime followed by transactionTime. A lone validTime axis belongs to the separately catalogued m-business-only formation and is not accepted by this active descriptor contract.

valueObjects.description: Embedded composite elements mapped to dialect-native structured-document columns via the m-core `json` neutral type (m-value-object/m-core; a deliberate deviation from Reladomo's column-flattening).

allOf.$comment: Optimistic-lock composition: a Transaction-Time Entity derives its optimistic key from its transactionTime start Attribute and must not declare an explicit optimisticLocking version Attribute.
A standalone Entity and a TPH root structurally own a table. Concrete-subtype table ownership depends on the root strategy and is enforced by whole-model formation.
Every abstract subtype and a TPCS root are tableless. A TPH root is the sole owner of the shared table.
Persistence is family-wide and root-owned; descendants inherit and cannot repeat it.

Conditional `attributes` requirement (m-inheritance / m-descriptor, resolved Q5): a non-inheritance entity MUST declare its own `attributes`. An inheritance participant MAY omit `attributes` — a concrete subtype that declares only inherited attributes has none of its own, and validation/lowering derives the full inherited chain from the ancestry. When `attributes` IS present it is still non-empty (the array schema).

At most one optimisticLocking attribute per entity (m-descriptor `attribute` / m-opt-lock "The version column"): the entity's OWN `attributes` array may CONTAIN at most one item with `optimisticLocking: true` (2020-12 `contains`/`minContains`/`maxContains`). `minContains: 0` is REQUIRED alongside `maxContains` — `contains` alone implies at least one match, which would wrongly force every entity to declare a version attribute; `minContains: 0` restores the flag's true optionality, so 0 or 1 both satisfy this, never more. This is a PER-ENTITY structural rule; the family-wide root-ownership rule (at most one across an inheritance family, declared only at the root) is the semantic `inheritance-optimistic-locking-not-root-owned` invariant a model-aware validator enforces (m-inheritance), not expressible here.

default.description: Optional typed default value in the declared type's wire encoding (m-descriptor "Value encodings"). Omission means NoDefault; an explicit null means DefaultValue(null) — the position's contractual null, never a value from any value space — and is legal for every declared type. The per-type conditionals below constrain the encoding structurally; scale agreement, integer range, the canonical-digit rules, and the canonical float rendering are semantic checks.

allOf.$comment: Primary-key generation is meaningful only for a primary-key Attribute. The normalized PrimaryKey sum type makes every non-key generation state unconstructible.

Explicit optimistic-lock version (m-opt-lock / DQ8b): an attribute-level `optimisticLocking: true` version is derived — initialized to `1` on insert and advanced to `observed + 1` on every update, gated in optimistic mode with `and <col> = ?` — so it MUST be an integral type (`int32`/`int64`). A temporal Entity locks via its Transaction-Time start (`tx_start`, physically `in_z`) instead and declares no explicit version Attribute (the Entity-level composition rule above forbids the combination), so this restriction never lands on a timestamp column.

Typed `default` encodings, keyed on the declared type (m-descriptor "Value encodings"). Each conditional admits null (DefaultValue(null)) alongside the declared type's encoding; `json` stays structurally unconstrained because its value space spans the JSON data model's structured content, and a bare `default: null` there already reads as DefaultValue(null) (m-core: a top-level null is not a Json value).

relationship.description: One branch of the closed defining/reverse relationship declaration union.

reverseOf.description: Entity reference plus defining relationship name, split at the final dot.

dependent.description: True when the target is owned by this entity (participates in cascade, m-cascade-delete).

orderBy.description: Optional ordering for a to-many relationship.

relationshipJoin.description: Structured mapping equality. Source is local; target carries the sole target Entity reference.

attributes.description: Ordered list of attribute names participating in the index.

unique.description: True for a unique index (enables the cache fast-path).

asOfAxis.description: One valid-time or transaction-time half-open interval over two local Timestamp Attributes.

valueObject.description: A top-level embedded composite stored in one structured-document column. Omitted column uses the occurrence name.

column.description: The single structured-document column the whole value object (attributes + nested value objects) is stored in. Top-level only — a nested value object never declares a column.

multiplicity.description: One composite or an ordered non-null collection in the same column.

attributes.description: Typed fields of this value object. Unlike entity attributes they carry no per-field column: every attribute lives inside the one structured-document column.

valueObjects.description: Value objects nested inside this one, to arbitrary depth. Stored in the SAME structured-document column — never extra columns, rows, or joins.

nestedValueObject.description: A storage-neutral nested occurrence inside its top-level ancestor's structured-document column.

multiplicity.description: One composite or an ordered non-null collection.

attributes.description: Typed fields of this nested value object; no per-field column.

valueObjects.description: Further-nested value objects, to arbitrary depth, in the same column.

valueObjectAttribute.description: A typed field of a value object. Unlike an entity `attribute` it carries NO per-field `column`: the whole value object (all attributes plus nested value objects) is stored in one structured-document column, so an inner field has no column of its own. Its declared m-core neutral type is normative for nested-predicate literal typing and casting (m-op-algebra/m-sql).

inheritance.description: Closed-tree inheritance. The root alone declares strategy, persistence, and asOfAxes. Under table-per-hierarchy the root owns the one shared table and tag column; descendants own no table. Under table-per-concrete-subtype abstract nodes are tableless and every concrete subtype owns a table.

strategy.description: The family mapping strategy, declared ONLY by the `root` (descendants inherit it). table-per-leaf and table-per-class are intentionally absent: the enum rejects them.

role.description: This entity's position in the closed tree: the abstract `root` that names the family and declares its strategy; an `abstract-subtype` interior node (tableless, rowless, addressable as a polymorphic position); or a `concrete-subtype`, the only instantiable, row-owning participant.

parent.description: For a non-root participant (`abstract-subtype` / `concrete-subtype`), the name of the entity it directly extends. REQUIRED for a non-root, FORBIDDEN for a root.

tag.description: table-per-hierarchy ROOT only: the discriminator column in the shared table distinguishing concrete subtypes. Declared once on the root (not repeated on descendants) and FORBIDDEN under table-per-concrete-subtype (no shared table). It is a framework-owned metadata column, NOT a declared entity attribute — abstract-target reads project it raw and materialize `familyVariant` from it (m-sql / m-case-format, resolved Q6).

column.description: The tag (discriminator) column name in the shared table.

tagValue.description: table-per-hierarchy CONCRETE-SUBTYPE only: the value the root's tag column carries for THIS concrete subtype's rows. Declared only on a `concrete-subtype`; whether it is REQUIRED depends on the family strategy (a cross-entity rule the root owns), so this per-entity schema leaves it optional and the semantic validator enforces its presence under table-per-hierarchy and its absence under table-per-concrete-subtype.

allOf.description: The `root` declares the family strategy and has NO parent; every non-root participant names its `parent` and does NOT redeclare the strategy.

The `tag` column lives on the table-per-hierarchy ROOT and nowhere else: REQUIRED on a `root` declaring `strategy: table-per-hierarchy`, FORBIDDEN otherwise (a table-per-concrete-subtype root, an abstract subtype, or a concrete subtype).
`tagValue` is a concrete subtype's discriminated value: only a `concrete-subtype` may declare it (a `root` / `abstract-subtype` MUST NOT).

pkGeneration.description: Primary-key generation strategy. Only the strategy keyword is required; sequence carries extra fields.
